title: Comella’s closes Melrose location after 13 years in business
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Comella’s closed its Melrose location on March 20 after 13 years due to operational and financial challenges.
The Melrose location was independently owned and the closure was a franchisee-level decision.
Comella’s continues to operate 10 locations across Massachusetts including in Belmont, Concord, and Hingham.

title: The most underrated movies of 2025 are streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Pamela Anderson and Liam Neeson star in the 2025 comedy 'The Naked Gun', available on Paramount+.
'The Ballad of Wallis Island' features Tim Key in a comedic role, streaming on Prime Video.
Danny Boyle's '28 Years Later', a sequel exploring post-Brexit themes, streams on Netflix.

title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 22-year-old man was fatally shot by Westminster police after allegedly threatening them with a knife.
The incident occurred at the man's home on South Ashburnham Road as police served an arrest warrant.
The officers involved are on administrative leave as the Worcester County DA and State Police investigate.

title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Rachel Dratch's reaction to a 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' clue about the Spanish Armada went viral on social media.
The hairdresser Eddie Cook mentioned the Spanish Armada before Dratch's appearance on 'Celebrity Jeopardy!', prompting her to look it up.
Dratch appeared in the 'Celebrity Jeopardy! All Stars' and discussed her viral moment on 'Late Night with Seth Meyers'.

title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Andrea Calleja to run 2026 Boston Marathon after surviving a ruptured brain aneurysm.
Calleja's marathon journey began in 2017 and includes New York, Berlin, Chicago, Tokyo, London, and Sydney.
She volunteers with the Brain Aneurysm Foundation, advocating for those facing similar health challenges.

title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: 261 at Para Maria pop-up bar opened in The Envoy Hotel, Seaport on March 19, runs until April 30.
The pop-up honors women's sports, features meals named after Kathrine Switzer, and hosts related events and games.
A portion of cocktail sales benefits Dream Big!, supporting sports equipment and training for low-income girls.

title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to repay over $224,000 for fraud involving pandemic funds and diverted nonprofit donations.
The former nonprofit leader was sentenced to six months home confinement, 100 hours community service, and four years probation.
Cannon-Grant and her late husband diverted donations and lied to obtain pandemic aid, using funds for personal expenses.

title: India-based Tanishq jewelry opens its first store in New England
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Tanishq, an Indian jewelry brand, opens its first New England store at 303 Turnpike Road, Westborough.
The 4,000 sq ft showroom offers over 5,000 designs, including bridal, festive, and everyday jewelry.
Raghuram S., India's consul general in Boston, attended the grand opening, highlighting Indian brand expansion in the U.S.

title: NH athlete is running Boston for the care that keeps her breathing
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Alison Clode, 50, from Portsmouth, NH, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Mass Eye and Ear research.
Clode receives care from Dr. Song at Mass Eye and Ear for subglottic stenosis, a condition affecting her breathing.
She aims to raise funds for ongoing research to improve diagnosis and management of breathing-related conditions.

title: A major North Shore office park is adding 60 new condos
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Cummings Properties is adding a 60-unit condominium building at the Cumming Center in Beverly to meet high demand.
The new Beverly Landing development reserves 80% of homes for residents aged 55 and older, with prices from $500,000 to $1.5 million.
Cummings Properties bought the site in 1996 and spent over $80 million transforming it into a mixed-use campus.
